purpose: Verify rule 4 (concurrency cap, session cache, fallback) behaviour of bitopro-market-intel v1.2.0. Simulated execution — not live HTTP.

scenarios.name: Same session fires 10 consecutive '市場概況' requests

timeline.user_prompt: 給我一份加密市場概況

expected.t=0: 4 external calls: T1 (Alternative.me), T2 (CoinGecko global), T3 (CoinGecko markets with ids=...), T4 (CoinGecko trending) — per rule 2 first batch [T1,T2,T3] then T4 as follow-up
expected.t=3..t=58: 0 external calls — all hit cache (TTL 60s). Response still shows Alternative.me + CoinGecko in footer (cache is transparent).
expected.t=62: T1 (TTL 60s hit @ t=62 still valid — fetched at t=0, age 62s = EXPIRED for T1/T2/T3/T4). So EXPECT re-fetch of all 4. But T1 F&G cache TTL is 60s; 62 > 60 → miss.
expected.t=65: 0 external calls — entries just refreshed at t=62 are valid (age 3s).
expected.t=90: Mixed: entries at t=62 age 28s → still cached → 0 external calls.
expected.footer_integrity: Every response must cite Alternative.me + CoinGecko regardless of cache status (rule: 'caching is transparent').

fail_if: Any turn between t=3 and t=58 triggers a new external request.
t=65 or t=90 triggers a new request (should be cached from t=62 refresh).
Any response omits the source footer because the data came from cache.
Total external calls > 10 (upper bound: 4 initial + 4 refresh @ t=62 + 0 or +2 margin for timing).

scenarios.name: Mock CoinGecko returning 429 for all T2/T3/T4/T5; verify fallback paths

setup.user_prompt_t0: 幫我看一下 5 個幣的市值排行（BTC/ETH/SOL/DOGE/ADA）+ 全球市值 + Fear & Greed
setup.user_prompt_t5: 再來看 CoinGecko 上今天 trending 裡有哪些是 BitoPro 的

t=0 behaviour.T2_global: CoinGecko 429 → read Retry-After=45 → ONE retry after honouring Retry-After? NO — rule says max 1 retry per tool per turn, but a 45s wait inside a single turn would block. Correct behaviour: do NOT auto-wait 45s mid-turn; go straight to fallback CoinPaprika /v1/global → 200 OK
t=0 behaviour.T3_rankings: CoinGecko 429 → fallback: call CoinPaprika /v1/tickers/{id} for 5 coins in batches of 3 → batch 1: btc-bitcoin, eth-ethereum, sol-solana (parallel); batch 2: doge-dogecoin, ada-cardano (parallel, after batch 1 completes)
t=0 behaviour.footer: 📌 數據來源：Alternative.me / CoinPaprika (CoinGecko 限流已切換)

t=5 behaviour.T4_trending: CoinGecko 429 → no CoinPaprika fallback exists for trending → report failure for trending specifically, do NOT fabricate
t=5 behaviour.user_message: should contain '⚠️ CoinGecko trending 暫時限流' or equivalent
t=5 behaviour.footer: No external source listed for trending (failed). Overall footer cites nothing new.

t=10 behaviour.user_message: should say public-company holdings data unavailable

fail_if: Any retry to CoinGecko more than once per tool per turn.
Auto-waiting 45s inside a single turn (blocks UX).
Footer claims 'CoinGecko' when only CoinPaprika returned data.
T4 trending fabricated from some other source.
T3 fallback fires > 3 CoinPaprika requests in parallel.
Fails to route 5-coin rankings through T3 batch first (cannot N+1 per-coin CoinGecko calls even without 429).

scenarios.name: Cache warm from happy path; then 429 hits on next turn

expected.t=0: Full fetch, cache populated for T1-T4 at t=0
expected.t=30: All entries still within 60s TTL → 0 external calls. CoinGecko 429 is IRRELEVANT because no request is made. Footer unchanged (Alternative.me + CoinGecko).

fail_if: Triggers external call at t=30 despite cache hit.
Mentions 429 or fallback at t=30 (should be invisible to user since cache covered).

runbook: 1. For each scenario, feed the SKILL.md (rules 2 and 4 are load-bearing) plus the setup to a runtime simulator.
2. The simulator emits an ordered call log: [(tool_id, endpoint, status, via_cache: bool, time)].
3. Compare against `expected`. Any item in `fail_if` → scenario fails.
